emergent_themes.description: Using delivery features (especially tracking) as psychological comfort and control mechanism during stressful periods
Having adult income but still relating to student value expectations and lifestyle constraints
Claiming to love cooking while simultaneously finding it burdensome during busy periods
Strong emotional aversion to grocery shopping leading to delivery preference despite cost concerns

coding_summary: P010 demonstrates sophisticated consumer behaviour as a mature student with full-time work. Her relationship with delivery is highly strategic - using it as a psychological safety net when her meal prep systems fail. She shows strong loyalty to platforms offering predictability (tracking) over price savings. Her psychotherapy background influences her language and self-awareness about decision-making patterns. Key tensions emerge between her aspirational cooking identity and practical constraints, and between her adult income and student value expectations.
coverage_gaps: RO4_post_graduation.does_the_value_equation_for_delivery_shi - P010 is currently a student, though mature, so no direct post-graduation comparison data available
RO3_emotional_social.is_there_stigma_around_solo_ordering_or_ - No evidence of stigma discussed; participant appears comfortable with solo ordering
